title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol enabling AI applications and agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The video covers MCP architecture, client-server communication patterns, JSON-RPC implementation, and deployment modes including local and remote configurations.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standardized protocol that lets AI applications and agents connect to external tools, APIs, databases, and services. It walks through MCP architecture and the client-server communication pattern, including the JSON-RPC implementation underneath. It also covers deployment modes, contrasting local and remote configurations. The relevance: MCP standardizes how agents and multi-agent systems reach external resources, reducing per-integration glue.
